Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. About memory pressure, lock contention, and Data-oriented Design 46 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. WebPKI and You 36 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Datahäxan 49 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. SBCL: A Sanely-Bootstrappable Common Lisp (2008) [pdf] 13 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Show HN: I built a tool that watches webpages and exposes changes as RSS 228 upvotes, 51 comments
  6. Tested: How Many Times Can a DVD±RW Be Rewritten? Methodology and Results 134 upvotes, 28 comments
  7. Against vibes: When is a generative model useful 82 upvotes, 16 comments
  8. Temporal: The 9-year journey to fix time in JavaScript 654 upvotes, 206 comments
  9. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans 3417 upvotes, 1276 comments
  10. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 532 upvotes, 182 comments
  11. Show HN: s@: decentralized social networking over static sites 240 upvotes, 92 comments
  12. Many SWE-bench-Passing PRs would not be merged 223 upvotes, 97 comments
  13. BitNet: 100B Param 1-Bit model for local CPUs 333 upvotes, 161 comments
  14. Iran-backed hackers claim wiper attack on medtech firm Stryker 140 upvotes, 61 comments
  15. Show HN: A context-aware permission guard for Claude Code 94 upvotes, 38 comments
  16. 5,200 holes carved into a Peruvian mountain left by an ancient economy 125 upvotes, 61 comments
  17. Faster asin() was hiding in plain sight 197 upvotes, 107 comments
  18. Google closes deal to acquire Wiz 279 upvotes, 167 comments
  19. Show HN: Klaus – OpenClaw on a VM, batteries included 139 upvotes, 80 comments
  20. Show HN: XLA-based array computing framework for R 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. Entities enabling scientific fraud at scale (2025) 286 upvotes, 195 comments
  22. NASA's DART spacecraft changed an asteroid's orbit around the sun 13 upvotes, 4 comments
  23. What Happens After You Die? (2016) 32 upvotes, 17 comments
  24. Personal Computer by Perplexity 147 upvotes, 119 comments
  25. I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job 279 upvotes, 253 comments
  26. Challenging the Single-Responsibility Principle 25 upvotes, 15 comments
  27. Returning to Rails in 2026 65 upvotes, 52 comments
  28. I'm glad the Anthropic fight is happening now 137 upvotes, 179 comments
  29. The MacBook Neo 509 upvotes, 838 comments
  30. Meticulous (YC S21) is hiring to redefine software dev 0 upvotes, 0 comments
